text: Beta Inc uses MongoDB Atlas for the primary document store backing the membership service.
Beta Inc runs the legacy billing service on MySQL 8 hosted on a single Google Cloud SQL instance.
Beta Inc's analytics pipeline lands events in BigQuery via Pub/Sub, partitioned daily.
Beta Inc stores user-uploaded files in Google Cloud Storage with a 90-day cold-tier policy.
Beta Inc's authentication is handled by Firebase Auth with Google and email providers enabled.
Beta Inc runs its workloads on Google Kubernetes Engine in europe-west1 with autopilot mode enabled.
Beta Inc's checkout flow integrates with Adyen as the payment processor across all markets.
Beta Inc uses Datadog for end-to-end observability across services, infra, and frontend RUM.
Beta Inc's primary frontend is a Next.js 14 app hosted on Vercel with the Edge runtime.
Beta Inc uses Algolia for in-app search across membership directories and content libraries.
Beta Inc keeps an internal admin dashboard on Retool, used by the customer ops team daily.
Beta Inc ships a mobile app for iOS and Android built with Flutter 3.16.
Beta Inc's email delivery uses Postmark for transactional and Customer.io for lifecycle campaigns.
Beta Inc's CDN is Fastly with image optimization rules for the membership profile pictures.
Beta Inc uses Looker as the BI tool, with semantic models defined per business domain.
Beta Inc's marketing automation runs on HubSpot for nurture sequences and Mailchimp for newsletters.
Beta Inc's support team uses Intercom for in-app messaging and a Zendesk fallback for email tickets.
Beta Inc runs Cloudflare Workers in front of the membership API for rate limiting and bot mitigation.
Beta Inc's developer team relies on GitHub Actions for CI and Pulumi for infrastructure as code.
Beta Inc keeps secrets in Google Secret Manager and syncs them into GKE via External Secrets Operator.
Beta Inc's data platform pipes BigQuery exports into Snowflake for cross-cloud reporting.
Beta Inc deploys its ML feature store on Feast running against BigQuery as the offline store.
Beta Inc's recommendation service uses TensorFlow 2.16 served via TensorFlow Serving on GKE.
Beta Inc's design system is published as an npm package called @beta/ui, versioned with changesets.
Beta Inc's CEO is Priya Anand, who co-founded the company in 2021.
Beta Inc's CTO is Theo Ferreira, joining from a senior engineering role at Booking.com in 2022.
Beta Inc is headquartered in Amsterdam with a remote-first policy across the EU and UK.
Beta Inc raised a Series A of $22M from Atomico and Felix Capital in late 2023.
Beta Inc operates a B2B membership platform for professional service firms in legal and accounting.
Beta Inc's largest customer segment by revenue is mid-size law firms in the UK and Netherlands.
Beta Inc's enterprise SSO offering supports SAML 2.0 with Okta and Azure AD reference setups.
Beta Inc has ISO 27001 certification renewed annually with a Big-Four audit partner.
Beta Inc runs WAF rules at the Cloudflare edge to block common SQL-injection and XSS patterns.
Beta Inc maintains a private Snyk policy that blocks PR merges on Critical or High vulnerabilities.
Beta Inc enforces signed commits for all main-branch merges across every service repo.
Beta Inc runs internal documentation in Notion with a strict tagging convention per business domain.
Beta Inc uses Linear for product and engineering issue tracking, replacing a legacy Jira instance in 2024.
Beta Inc's design team uses Figma with the dev-mode handoff workflow into Storybook.
Beta Inc runs internal communications on Slack, with a strict no-DM policy for incidents.
Beta Inc's HR and payroll are managed on Personio across all five EU entities.
To deploy a new version of the Beta membership service, push a tag to the beta-membership repo; GitHub Actions promotes to GKE via Pulumi.
To investigate slow Beta queries, open the Datadog APM service map and drill into the membership-api span.
To refresh a Beta Looker dashboard from raw BigQuery data, trigger the dbt cloud job named beta-daily-refresh.
To rotate a Beta Firebase Auth signing key, run the rotate-fb-jwks workflow in the beta-platform repo.
For Beta on-call incidents, the primary rotation pages via PagerDuty service beta-prod with a 5-minute escalation.
To onboard a new Beta engineer, run the beta-bootstrap.sh script to install Pulumi, gcloud, and the asdf-managed Node and Python toolchains.
To debug a Beta Algolia ranking issue, replay the indexed payload locally via the algolia-replay CLI.
For Beta data exports, use the beta-data-export.ts script with BigQuery service-account credentials from Secret Manager.
To run the Beta Flutter app locally, use FVM with the pinned Flutter 3.16 channel for parity with CI.
To trigger a Beta hotfix release, branch from main, merge with the hotfix label, and tag a patch version.
To investigate a Beta payment failure, query the Adyen dashboard with the merchant reference and cross-reference the membership service log.
To re-issue a Beta enterprise SSO certificate, generate a new IdP metadata file and upload it via the beta admin console.
For Beta GDPR deletion requests, run the beta-gdpr-delete script and confirm both MongoDB and BigQuery deletion logs.
Beta Inc currently has 18,400 active paid seats across all customer accounts.
Beta Inc's membership service p99 latency is around 220ms after the November 2025 caching rollout.
Beta Inc's monthly Google Cloud spend is around $19k, dominated by GKE Autopilot and BigQuery slots.
Beta Inc's recurring revenue grew 38% year over year, ending Q4 2025 at €11.2M ARR.
Beta Inc's enterprise pipeline currently shows 12 active deals over €100k ARR each.
Beta Inc rolled out the new dark-mode design across the Next.js app last week with no rollback incidents.
Beta Inc shipped the SSO-for-enterprise feature on schedule in November 2025.
Beta Inc closed the Magnusson & Wright legal firm as the largest customer of Q4 2025 at €260k ARR.
Beta Inc's NPS score climbed from 42 to 51 between Q2 and Q4 2025 across the membership cohort.
Beta Inc had one Sev-2 incident in November caused by a Cloudflare Workers rule misconfiguration; resolved in 28 minutes.
Beta Inc's data team migrated the dbt project from version 1.5 to 1.7 last week with no broken downstream models.
Beta Inc's Q4 2025 hiring plan is 8 net-new engineers, of which 4 have signed offers as of last week.
Beta Inc learned that Vercel's Edge runtime cold starts are negligible in production traffic but spike in low-volume preview environments.
Beta Inc found that switching from REST to tRPC inside the Next.js app eliminated a whole class of typing bugs at the API boundary.
Beta Inc learned that running BigQuery materialized views for hot dashboards cut Looker query latency by an order of magnitude.
Beta Inc discovered that Algolia ranks better when the synonyms dictionary is curated per market rather than globally.
Beta Inc's mobile team learned that Flutter's image caching needed a custom eviction policy to avoid OOM crashes on low-end Android devices.
Beta Inc learned that Adyen's notification webhooks are not strictly ordered, so idempotent processing is mandatory.
Beta Inc found that pre-warming TensorFlow Serving containers before a deploy avoided the first-request spike entirely.
Beta Inc's growth team learned that enterprise demos convert 4x better when scheduled within 24 hours of the inbound request.
Beta Inc learned that the Notion documentation discoverability dropped sharply over 500 pages; they introduced a curated index per domain.
Beta Inc's security team learned that signed commits caught two real impersonation attempts via stolen GitHub tokens within the first quarter.
Beta Inc's SRE team found that GKE Autopilot's scheduler latency was the bottleneck during burst traffic; pre-provisioning helped.
Beta Inc learned that running Datadog log retention at 7 days for app logs and 30 for audit logs was the sweet spot for cost and forensics.
Beta Inc found that customer-success playbooks tied to NPS dips reduced churn by roughly 0.6 percentage points per quarter.
Beta Inc learned that the Customer.io lifecycle emails outperformed Mailchimp newsletters by 2.7x on click-through for re-engagement.
Beta Inc found that running their Pulumi state in Google Cloud Storage with versioning enabled gave them painless rollback after a bad infra change.
